_note: Real-network smoke list — NOT part of npm test / CI. Run manually: node test/live-smoke.js or npm run smoke. WAF status changes over time; treat failures as signals to investigate, not hard regressions.
_waf_caveat: WAF decisions (Akamai, Cloudflare, etc.) are IP-reputation-based. The same URL may pass from some IPs and block from others. A change here is NOT automatically a code regression.

urls.description: WordPress site with jQuery. The regression case: must report WordPress + jQuery in stack.

expected.stackNote: Both WordPress and jQuery must appear in techStack.detected

urls.description: Akamai datacenter-IP block. Server-side HTTP fetch from a DO function IP will be 403.

expected.stackNote: No tech stack detectable from WAF response

urls.description: Shopify marketing site. Should detect Shopify in the stack.
Next.js official site — built with Next.js, should detect it.
Google web.dev — Eleventy or similar static site, no commerce signals.

expected.stackNote: Service/content site — must never be flagged missing Product schema

urls.description: WordPress.org — WordPress CMS. Should detect WordPress in stack.
US SSA government site. GovernmentOrganization schema or plain HTML. Must NEVER flag missing Product schema.

expected.stackNote: Government site — Product schema finding must be absent

urls.description: Cloudflare marketing site — usually readable but may self-challenge. React or Next.js expected.

expected.stackNote: If readable: React or Next.js expected. If WAF: staticWasBlocked must be true, never score 0 as verdict.

urls.description: Heavy bot protection — likely Akamai or similar. Expect blocked_by_waf from a datacenter IP.
Vercel marketing site — Next.js, Organization schema expected.
